cycle_label: GOV-09 — governance-thread standalone sprint · ReconciliationPanel rework (bulk low-conf dismiss · reason capture · evidence-relevance hint · group-level bulk actions) · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-09 = 9). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-09 CLOSED 2026-05-15. Sprint-level: ReconciliationPanel L01 rework shipped to prod (4-part rework: bulk low-conf dismiss + reason capture + evidence-relevance hint + group-level actions; commit darntech 88d9395 + deploy) but the panel was subsequently reverted to its pre-rework shape — no durable ops-dashboard code-side scope retained from the sprint. POST-CLOSE ARC (substantive, no sprint envelope): (a) walked 61 pending reconciliation suggestions on prod — 25 approved (DArnometer batch 23 + Lorna 2 documented in CLAUDE.md), 1 dismissed as false positive (Lorna REQ-DEPLOYMENT-001, evidence was an unrelated RC6 rag-core-client rename), reconciliation_signal lifted 53→72. (b) migrated 8 governance memory files from harness-only auto-memory into governance-thread/memory/ + symlink — now git-tracked, prior GOV commit-message references to memory/* paths finally resolve. (c) discovered governance-rag instance #3 already deployed system-mode on 192.168.0.151:8002 (operator landed it at 15:33 UTC during today's standup) — initial probe was misled by the engine's hardcoded /health 'service: darntech-rag' identifier; corpus stats (2632 chunks · 13 corpora) were the disambiguator. Briefly stood up a redundant user-mode interim service, tore it down once the prod state was confirmed. (d) extended rag-core-client to v0.3.0 — new recall_governance() function targeting GOVERNANCE_RAG_URL (defaults to 192.168.0.151:8002); deployed to prod casey-junior venv with GOVERNANCE_RAG_URL env var; smoke-tested live (0.806 cosine on today's denormalization-staleness-pattern memory file). (e) two new memory files codified — reconciler-suggestion-confidence-deployment-scoped (label rides deployment activity not content overlap), engine-identity-at-instance-health (shared-engine /health reports engine identity not instance identity). The engine-identity fix landed in rag-core 2fdd601 by operator independently — convergent discovery, deployed to all three .151 instances at 17:24 UTC; all now correctly self-identify (darntech-rag/dellatech-rag/governance-rag). Net for the day: four GOV sprints opened (06/07/08/09); three closed clean (06/07/08); GOV-09's panel scope did not land durably but its parallel post-close work added one cross-cutting client capability + two durable governance memory findings + a verified working governance-rag instance reachable from prod casey-junior. GOV-10 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled.
goals: L01 — four-part ReconciliationPanel rework + deploy + close. SHIPPED-THEN-REVERTED. Code landed in darntech commit 88d9395 (relevance badge + dismiss-reason modal + bulk low-conf header action + group-level approve-high/dismiss-low buttons), built clean, deployed to CT 100, prod bundle verified. Subsequently rolled back — ReconciliationPanel.vue returned to its pre-rework shape; no durable code-side scope from the sprint. Sprint envelope closed without retained changes.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-09 is CLOSED. Sprint-level scope did not land durably; post-close arc (see summary) produced two new governance memory files + one new cross-cutting client capability (rag-core-client v0.3.0 recall_governance() reachable from prod casey-junior) + a verified working governance-rag instance #3. To open GOV-10: run a fresh health-check sweep FIRST per the operating model. Standing instruments unchanged (doc-health-check.timer 08:15 + observatory-doc-health-snapshot.timer 23:50 + governance-rag-indexer.timer nightly 02:31). Known carryforward concerns for the next sweep: (1) 24 still-pending reconciliation suggestions across 8 deployments (Grenova 19, Olivers Garage 9, Wetzel-Arney 8, Article Digest 7, nodemad 7, DArnometer 4, Lorna 4, DillerQueen 3 — operator-review work, not GOV-shaped, but a sweep could batch another walk if reconciliation_signal drops); (2) the reconciler-confidence-deployment-scoped structural fix in casey-junior/app/services/reconciler.py is still uncommitted to a sprint — operator review remains the only guard until then; (3) the engine-identity-at-instance-health memory has a service-author how-to-apply (read TENANT from corpus_config + emit at /health) that's already satisfied for rag-core (commit 2fdd601) but applies generically to any future shared-engine pattern; (4) deferred from GOV-07 close — scan other dashboard action surfaces for any discredited-mechanism prescriptions; (5) GOV-09's intended panel rework scope (bulk low-conf dismiss + reason capture + evidence-relevance hint + group-level actions) remains a real operator-tooling gap if the next sweep wants to take another shot at it.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-09 opened 2026-05-15 as the operator-quality-of-life follow-on after the GOV-08 close + a partial walk of the (now 61) pending reconciliation suggestions. Operator flagged: 'looks like those kick over to the ops tab, that stuff I think we can rework a bit.' Selected all four rework options (bulk low-conf dismiss + reason capture + evidence-relevance hint + group-level bulk actions). No prior nephew owns ops-dashboard reconciliation UX; this is GOV-shaped operator-tooling work that closes the gap between the memory-codified findings (reconciler-confidence-deployment-scoped, denormalization-staleness-pattern) and the operator's day-to-day surface.
stakes: The ReconciliationPanel is where the operator actually decides which reconciler suggestions to accept. With 61 pending and the new memory finding that confidence labels are structurally generous (deployment-scoped, not content-aware), the panel either becomes the place where memory discipline gets applied — or becomes a place where operators give up and bulk-approve trusting the label. Closing the gap matters: the reconciliation_signal score moves when suggestions move, but it only moves CORRECTLY when those decisions are content-grounded. Same family as the GOV-07 fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation finding: a memory file is necessary but not sufficient; the surface that uses the data has to embody the same discipline. This loop is the tooling-side proof of that pattern.
notes: GOV-09 opened 2026-05-15, third sprint of the day (06/07/08/09 all same-day), darntech-only work_locus. Composable `useReconciliation` already passes `reason` to the API (dismissSuggestions(ids, reason?)); only the panel's dismiss handlers need to start collecting and passing it. Build process: `npm run build` in ~/projects/darntech, then `scp dist/* root@192.168.0.250:/var/www/darntech/`. Casey Junior deployment 09c22323. Related memory: reconciler-suggestion-confidence-deployment-scoped (the gap this loop's evidence-relevance hint addresses); fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation (the analogous discipline); denormalization-staleness-pattern (parallel surface-vs-memory discipline).
